text.title: frameworks Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for frameworks. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Pick the smallest subset of frameworks that answers the current decision — never run all eight at once. Decision modes: idea, validate, size, position, pivot.
rule.rationale: Running all eight frameworks on one idea is framework theater. Agents default to exhaustive execution; the router constraint prevents this and keeps output actionable.

rule.statement: Lock the ICP in one sentence before any framework runs; reuse that sentence verbatim across all artifacts.
rule.rationale: Frameworks produce inconsistent outputs when each run re-infers a slightly different ICP. One locked sentence prevents drift and enables downstream SDD agents to ingest artifacts directly.

rule.statement: Apply all 7 Ps systematically, generate 2-3 ideas per P, and tag each idea with its underlying user-pain-id to prevent duplication across Pain/Profession/People cells.
rule.rationale: Agents fill Pain/Profession/People with restatements of the same idea. Requiring a unique user-pain-id per idea forces structural diversity across the seven prompts.

rule.statement: For agent runs, skip "What do you find yourself building for yourself?" — agents cannot honestly answer this and will invent a project. Use the other three questions and pipe in real user history if available.
rule.rationale: Paul Graham questions reward introspection, not aggregation. LLMs trained on generic startup content will produce plausible but fabricated answers to introspective prompts.

rule.statement: Validate each Paul Graham answer with 2-3 other people before treating it as a market signal, and check whether solutions already exist.
rule.rationale: Personal pain points are necessary but not sufficient for a market. A solution that already exists at reasonable cost means the problem is solved, not an opportunity.

rule.statement: Score each of five criteria 1-10, require a one-line justification AND a cited source URL for market-size and competition columns before accepting the score.
rule.rationale: A 50/50 niche score with zero primary research is worse than no score — it legitimizes guesses. Citations force the agent to ground numbers in real evidence.

rule.statement: Apply all five validation criteria (frequency, intensity, WTP, search volume, competition) before recommending pursuit; the presence of competitors is a green flag, not a red flag — it confirms the problem exists.
rule.rationale: No competitors usually means no market, not a blue ocean. Absence of search volume means people are not actively looking for a solution. Both are failure signals.

rule.statement: Write the functional JTBD statement first using the "When/I want/So I can" template with a verifiable outcome — reject any outcome phrased as an adjective ("feel empowered") and replace with a measurable verb ("invoice sent in <90s, paid within 14 days").
rule.rationale: JTBD statements drift into wishful copywriting when outcomes are unmeasurable. Verifiable outcomes anchor product decisions and can be tested in user research.

rule.statement: Triangulate market size with three methods (top-down, bottom-up, competitor-based); do not average if the spread is greater than 2x — report the range and flag the divergence instead.
rule.rationale: Averaging incompatible methods produces false precision. A 2x spread signals that one method's assumptions are wrong; averaging hides the problem and misleads investors.

rule.statement: Round aggressively — $8.3B is fake precision, $8B is honest. Use two significant figures maximum for TAM/SAM.
rule.rationale: Market sizing compounds errors across four multipliers. Reporting four significant figures implies an accuracy that the underlying data does not support.

rule.statement: Cap competitor analysis at 5-7 named direct players plus one explicit "long tail" row; tag the rest as "long tail" with an estimated count.
rule.rationale: Agents over-list adjacent products to appear thorough. A table of 20 competitors is less useful than a tight table of 6 with deeper analysis per row.

rule.statement: Check VPC fit against actual user quotes (cite interview ID per row), not assumed pains/gains — agents over-claim fit when both sides of the canvas are invented.
rule.rationale: A VPC always "fits" if the agent invented both customer profile and value map. Requiring interview citations per pain-reliever and gain-creator row forces the canvas to be grounded in evidence.

rule.statement: Cite a URL with retrieval date on every external number. LLM-only cells are explicitly tagged "estimate".
rule.rationale: LLMs hallucinate framework cells fluently. Without a citation requirement the niche matrix becomes confidently wrong. The "estimate" tag signals which numbers need validation before acting on them.

rule.statement: Require two independent scorers (or two agent runs) for niche scoring; average only if within 2 points on each criterion.
rule.rationale: Identical inputs to two agent runs produce 15+ point spread. Two runs with averaged scores are more reliable than one run treated as ground truth.
